debug_info.goal:
U V : Type
⊢ U ∼ V ↔ ∃ f g, f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id

failed_attempts:
by
  constructor
  · -- Prove the forward direction: U ∼ V implies the existence of f and g
    intro h
    have h₁ : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := by
      -- Use the fact that U ∼ V to get an equivalence
      have h₂ : U ∼ V := h
      exact ⟨h₂⟩
    obtain ⟨e⟩ := h₁
    refine' ⟨e, e.symm, ?_, ?_⟩
    · -- Prove f ∘ g = id
      funext x
      simp [e.symm_apply_apply]
    · -- Prove g ∘ f = id
      funext x
      simp [e.apply_symm_apply]
  · -- Prove the reverse direction: existence of f and g implies U ∼ V
    rintro ⟨f, g, hfg, hgf⟩
    -- Use the given functions to construct an equivalence
    have h₁ : Function.Bijective f := by
      constructor
      · -- Prove f is injective
        intro a b h
        have h₂ : g (f a) = g (f b) := by rw [h]
        have h₃ : g ∘ f = id := hgf
        have h₄ : (g ∘ f) a = (g ∘ f) b := by rw [h₂]
        have h₅ : (g ∘ f) a = a := by
          have h₆ : (g ∘ f) a = id a := by
            rw [hgf]
          simp at h₆ ⊢
          exact h₆
        have h₆ : (g ∘ f) b = b := by
          have h₇ : (g ∘ f) b = id b := by
            rw [hgf]
          simp at h₇ ⊢
          exact h₇
        simp [h₅, h₆] at h₄ ⊢
        <;> aesop
      · -- Prove f is surjective
        intro b
        have h₂ : f (g b) = b := by
          have h₃ : (f ∘ g) b = b := by
            have h₄ : f ∘ g = id := hfg
            rw [h₄]
            <;> simp
          simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h₃
        exact ⟨g b, h₂⟩
    -- Construct the equivalence using the bijective function
    have h₂ : U ≃ V := {
      toFun := f
      invFun := g
      left_inv := by
        funext x
        have h₃ : (g ∘ f) x = x := by
          have h₄ : g ∘ f = id := hgf
          rw [h₄]
          <;> simp
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h₃
      right_inv := by
        funext x
        have h₃ : (f ∘ g) x = x := by
          have h₄ : f ∘ g = id := hfg
          rw [h₄]
          <;> simp
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h₃
    }
    exact h₂

  constructor
  · -- Forward direction: U ∼ V implies there exist f and g as specified.
    intro h
    obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
    refine' ⟨f, hf.symm, _⟩
    constructor
    · -- Prove f ∘ hf.symm = id
      ext x
      have h1 := congr_fun (hf.symm_apply_apply x) x
      simp at h1 ⊢
      <;> simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
      <;> aesop
    · -- Prove hf.symm ∘ f = id
      ext x
      have h1 := congr_fun (hf.apply_symm_apply x) x
      simp at h1 ⊢
      <;> simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
      <;> aesop
  · -- Backward direction: There exist f and g as specified implies U ∼ V.
    rintro ⟨f, g, hfg, hgf⟩
    refine' ⟨f, _⟩
    -- We need to prove that f is a bijection.
    have h1 : Function.Bijective f := by
      constructor
      · -- Prove that f is injective.
        intro x1 x2 h
        have h2 := congr_fun hgf x1
        have h3 := congr_fun hgf x2
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h2 h3
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
      · -- Prove that f is surjective.
        intro y
        have h2 := congr_fun hfg y
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h2
        -- Use the inverse function g to find a preimage of y.
        refine' ⟨g y, _⟩
        have h3 := congr_fun hgf (g y)
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h3 ⊢
        <;> simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
    -- Since f is bijective, we can use it to construct an equivalence.
    exact Equiv.ofBijective f h1

by
  constructor
  · intro h
    -- Use the fact that U ∼ V to get a bijection between U and V
    obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
    have h1 : ∃ (g : V → U), g ∘ f = id := by
      -- Since f is bijective, it has a left inverse
      refine' ⟨f.symm, _⟩
      simp [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
    have h2 : ∃ (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id := by
      -- Since f is bijective, it has a right inverse
      refine' ⟨f.symm, _⟩
      simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
    -- Combine the two inverses to get the desired pair of functions
    obtain ⟨g1, hg1⟩ := h1
    obtain ⟨g2, hg2⟩ := h2
    refine' ⟨f, g2, _⟩
    -- Verify that the composed functions are identity
    constructor
    · -- Verify f ∘ g2 = id
      have h3 : f ∘ g2 = id := hg2
      exact h3
    · -- Verify g2 ∘ f = id
      have h4 : g2 ∘ f = id := by
        calc
          g2 ∘ f = g2 ∘ f := rfl
          _ = id := by
            -- Use the fact that g1 ∘ f = id and g2 ∘ f = id to show g2 = g1
            have h5 : g2 ∘ f = id := by
              calc
                g2 ∘ f = g2 ∘ f := rfl
                _ = id := hg2
            have h6 : g1 ∘ f = id := hg1
            -- Since f is bijective, g2 = g1
            have h7 : g2 = g1 := by
              have h8 : g2 ∘ f = g1 ∘ f := by
                rw [h5, h6]
              have h9 : Function.Injective f := hf.injective
              have h10 : g2 = g1 := by
                have h11 : g2 ∘ f = g1 ∘ f := h8
                have h12 : Function.Injective (fun g : V → U => g ∘ f) := by
                  intro g1 g2 h
                  have h13 : g1 ∘ f = g2 ∘ f := h
                  have h14 : Function.Injective f := hf.injective
                  have h15 : g1 = g2 := by
                    funext x
                    have h16 : f (hf.symm x) = x := by
                      apply Equiv.apply_symm_apply
                    have h17 : g1 x = g2 x := by
                      calc
                        g1 x = g1 (f (hf.symm x)) := by rw [h16]
                        _ = (g1 ∘ f) (hf.symm x) := by rfl
                        _ = (g2 ∘ f) (hf.symm x) := by rw [h13]
                        _ = g2 (f (hf.symm x)) := by rfl
                        _ = g2 x := by rw [h16]
                    exact h17
                  exact h15
                exact h12 h11
              exact h10
            rw [h7]
            exact hg1
            <;> simp_all
          _ = id := by rfl
      exact h4
  · -- Prove the reverse direction: if there exist f : U → V and g : V → U such that f ∘ g = id and g ∘ f = id, then U ∼ V
    rintro ⟨f, g, hg, hf⟩
    -- Use the fact that f and g are inverses of each other to construct an equivalence
    refine' ⟨Equiv.ofBijective f ⟨_, _⟩⟩
    · -- Prove that f is injective
      intro a b h
      have h1 : g (f a) = a := by
        have h2 : g ∘ f = id := hf
        have h3 : (g ∘ f) a = id a := by rw [h2]
        simp at h3
        exact h3
      have h4 : g (f b) = b := by
        have h2 : g ∘ f = id := hf
        have h3 : (g ∘ f) b = id b := by rw [h2]
        simp at h3
        exact h3
      have h5 : g (f a) = g (f b) := by rw [h]
      have h6 : a = b := by
        calc
          a = g (f a) := by rw [h1]
          _ = g (f b) := by rw [h5]
          _ = b := by rw [h4]
      exact h6
    · -- Prove that f is surjective
      intro v
      have h1 : f (g v) = v := by
        have h2 : f ∘ g = id := hg
        have h3 : (f ∘ g) v = id v := by rw [h2]
        simp at h3
        exact h3
      exact ⟨g v, by rw [h1]⟩

      have h3 : g (f y) = y := by simpa using congr_fun hg y
      -- If f x = f y, then g (f x) = g (f y), so x = y.
      have h4 : g (f x) = g (f y) := by rw [hxy]
      simp_all

    · -- Prove f ∘ g = id

    · -- Prove g ∘ f = id
      funext x
      simp [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
  · -- Backward direction: existence of f and g implies U ∼ V
    intro h
    obtain ⟨f, g, h₁, h₂⟩ := h
    -- Use f to construct an equivalence
    have h₃ : Function.Bijective f := by
      constructor
      · -- Injective: g ∘ f = id implies f is injective
        intro a₁ a₂ h₄
        have h₅ : g (f a₁) = g (f a₂) := by rw [h₄]
        have h₆ : (g ∘ f) a₁ = (g ∘ f) a₂ := by simpa using h₅
        have h₇ : (g ∘ f) a₁ = a₁ := by
          have h₈ : g ∘ f = id := h₂
          rw [h₈]
          <;> simp
        have h₈ : (g ∘ f) a₂ = a₂ := by
          have h₉ : g ∘ f = id := h₂
          rw [h₉]
          <;> simp
        rw [h₇, h₈] at h₆
        exact h₆
      · -- Surjective: f ∘ g = id implies f is surjective
        intro b
        have h₃ : f (g b) = b := by
          have h₄ : f ∘ g = id := h₁
          have h₅ : (f ∘ g) b = b := by
            rw [h₄]
            <;> simp
          simpa using h₅
        exact ⟨g b, h₃⟩
    -- Construct an equivalence from bijective f
    exact ⟨Equiv.ofBijective f h₃⟩

by
  have h_forward : U ∼ V → (∃ (f : U → V) (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id) := by
    intro h
    have h₁ : ∃ (f : U → V) (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id := by
      -- Given an equivalence h : U ∼ V, we take f = h and g = h.symm
      refine' ⟨h, h.symm, _, _⟩
      -- Verify f ∘ g = id
      <;> ext
      <;> simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
      <;> rfl
    exact h₁
  
  have h_backward : (∃ (f : U → V) (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id) → U ∼ V := by
    rintro ⟨f, g, hfg, hgf⟩
    have h_injective : Function.Injective f := by
      -- Prove that f is injective using the left inverse g ∘ f = id
      have h : g ∘ f = id := hgf
      have h₁ : Function.Injective f := by
        intro a b h₂
        have h₃ : g (f a) = g (f b) := by rw [h₂]
        have h₄ : g (f a) = a := by
          have h₅ : (g ∘ f) a = a := by
            rw [h]
            <;> simp
          exact h₅
        have h₅ : g (f b) = b := by
          have h₆ : (g ∘ f) b = b := by
            rw [h]
            <;> simp
          exact h₆
        rw [h₄] at h₃
        rw [h₅] at h₃
        exact h₃
      exact h₁
    have h_surjective : Function.Surjective f := by
      -- Prove that f is surjective using the right inverse f ∘ g = id
      have h : f ∘ g = id := hfg
      have h₁ : Function.Surjective f := by
        intro y
        use g y
        have h₂ : (f ∘ g) y = y := by
          rw [h]
          <;> simp
        exact h₂
      exact h₁
    -- Construct the equivalence using the bijective function f
    exact Equiv.ofBijective f ⟨h_injective, h_surjective⟩
  
  apply Iff.intro
  · intro h
    exact h_forward h
  · intro h
    exact h_backward h

      have h₂ : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := by
        simpa [Equiv.nonempty_iff] using h

      obtain ⟨e⟩ := h₂
      -- Define f and g using the bijection and its inverse
      refine' ⟨e, e.symm, _⟩
      constructor
      · -- Prove that f ∘ g = id
        ext x
        simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
      · -- Prove that g ∘ f = id
        ext x
        simp [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
    exact h₁
  · -- Prove the reverse direction: if there exist f and g such that f ∘ g = id and g ∘ f = id, then U ∼ V
    intro h
    classical
    obtain ⟨f, g, hf, hg⟩ := h
    -- Use the functions f and g to construct the bijection
    have h₁ : U ≃ V := by
      -- Show that f is a bijection
      refine' ⟨f, g, _, _, _⟩ <;> simp_all [Function.leftInverse_iff, Function.rightInverse_iff]
      <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try
        {
          ext x y
          have h₂ := congr_fun hf x
          have h₃ := congr_fun hf y
          simp at h₂ h₃
          <;>
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
          <;>
          aesop
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          ext x
          have h₂ := congr_fun hf x
          simp at h₂
          <;>
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
          <;>
          aesop
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          ext x
          have h₂ := congr_fun hg x
          simp at h₂
          <;>
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
    -- Use the bijection to conclude that U ∼ V
    exact ⟨h₁⟩

  constructor
  · intro h
    have h₁ : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := ⟨h⟩
    have h₂ : ∃ (f : U → V) (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id := by
      classical
      cases' h₁ with e
      refine' ⟨e, e.symm, _⟩
      constructor <;> ext <;> simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
    exact h₂
  · intro h
    have h₁ : ∃ (f : U → V) (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id := h
    have h₂ : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := by
      classical
      cases' h₁ with f g hfg
      have h₃ : ∀ (x : U), g (f x) = x := by
        intro x
        have h₄ := congr_fun (hfg.2) x
        simp at h₄ ⊢
        <;> simp_all
      have h₄ : ∀ (x : V), f (g x) = x := by
        intro x
        have h₅ := congr_fun (hfg.1) x
        simp at h₅ ⊢
        <;> simp_all
      refine' ⟨Equiv.mk f g _ _⟩ <;> simp_all [Function.LeftInverse, Function.RightInverse]
      <;> aesop
    exact Nonempty.some h₂

  have h_imp : U ∼ V → (∃ (f : U → V) (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id) := by
    intro h
    -- Extract the equivalence from the hypothesis
    rcases h with ⟨f, g, h₁, h₂⟩
    -- Use the forward and backward functions of the equivalence
    refine' ⟨f, g, _⟩
    -- Verify the required identities
    constructor
    · -- Prove f ∘ g = id
      funext x
      rw [Function.comp_apply]
      have h₃ := congr_fun h₁ x
      simp_all [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
    · -- Prove g ∘ f = id
      funext x
      rw [Function.comp_apply]
      have h₃ := congr_fun h₂ x
      simp_all [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
  
  have h_conv : (∃ (f : U → V) (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id) → U ∼ V := by
    intro h
    -- Extract the functions f and g from the hypothesis
    rcases h with ⟨f, g, h₁, h₂⟩
    -- Construct an equivalence using f and g
    refine' ⟨f, g, _, _⟩
    · -- Prove that f ∘ g = id implies the left inverse condition
      funext x
      have h₃ := congr_fun h₁ x
      simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₃ ⊢
      <;> simp_all
    · -- Prove that g ∘ f = id implies the right inverse condition
      funext x
      have h₃ := congr_fun h₂ x
      simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₃ ⊢
      <;> simp_all
  
  constructor
  · -- Prove the forward direction: U ∼ V → ∃ f g, f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id
    intro h
    exact h_imp h
  · -- Prove the backward direction: (∃ f g, f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id) → U ∼ V
    intro h
    exact h_conv h

    have h₂ : Function.Bijective f := hf
    have h₃ : Function.Surjective f := h₂.2
    have h₄ : Function.Injective f := h₂.1
    -- Use the inverse function to construct g.
    have h₅ : ∃ (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id := by
      -- Since f is surjective, we can define g as a right inverse.
      have h₅₁ : ∃ (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id := by
        classical
        have h₅₂ : ∀ (y : V), ∃ (x : U), f x = y := by
          intro y
          exact h₃ y
        choose g hg using h₅₂
        refine' ⟨g, _⟩
        ext y
        simp [hg]
      exact h₅₁
    obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h₅
    have h₆ : ∃ (g : V → U), f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id := by
      -- We need to show that g is also a left inverse.
      have h₆₁ : g ∘ f = id := by
        have h₆₂ : Function.Injective f := h₄
        have h₆₃ : Function.Surjective f := h₃
        have h₆₄ : ∀ (x : U), g (f x) = x := by
          intro x
          have h₆₅ : f (g (f x)) = f x := by
            have h₆₆ : f (g (f x)) = (f ∘ g) (f x) := rfl
            rw [h₆₆]
            rw [hg]
            <;> simp
          have h₆₇ : g (f x) = x := by
            apply h₆₂
            rw [h₆₅]
            <;> simp
          exact h₆₇
        funext x
        exact h₆₄ x
      refine' ⟨g, hg, _⟩
      exact h₆₁
    obtain ⟨g', hg'₁, hg'₂⟩ := h₆
    refine' ⟨f, g', _, _⟩ <;> simp_all [Function.comp]
    <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try simp_all) <;>
    (try aesop)
  · -- Prove the reverse direction: if there exist f and g as required, then U ∼ V.
    rintro ⟨f, g, hfg, hgf⟩
    have h₁ : Function.Bijective f := by
      constructor
      · -- Prove that f is injective.
        have h₂ : g ∘ f = id := hgf
        have h₃ : Function.Injective (g ∘ f) := by
          rw [h₂]
          exact Function.injective_id
        exact Function.Injective.comp h₃ g
      · -- Prove that f is surjective.
        have h₂ : f ∘ g = id := hfg
        have h₃ : Function.Surjective (f ∘ g) := by
          rw [h₂]
          exact Function.surjective_id
        exact Function.Surjective.comp h₃ f
    -- Since f is bijective, we can construct an equivalence.
    exact ⟨f, by simpa [Equiv.bijective] using h₁⟩

by
  constructor
  · -- Prove the forward direction: if U ∼ V, then there exist f and g with the required properties.
    intro h
    -- Extract the equivalence from the hypothesis.
    rcases h with ⟨f, hf⟩
    -- Obtain the inverse function and the required properties.
    refine' ⟨f, hf.invFun, _, _⟩ <;>
    (try simp_all [Function.LeftInverse, Function.RightInverse, Function.comp]) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try
      {
        ext x
        simp_all [Function.LeftInverse, Function.RightInverse, Function.comp]
        <;> aesop
      }) <;>
    (try
      {
        ext x
        simp_all [Function.LeftInverse, Function.RightInverse, Function.comp]
        <;> aesop
      })
  · -- Prove the backward direction: if there exist f and g with the required properties, then U ∼ V.
    rintro ⟨f, g, hfg, hgf⟩
    -- Show that f is an equivalence by verifying it is bijective.
    refine' ⟨f, _⟩
    -- Provide the inverse function and verify the required properties.
    refine' ⟨g, ?_, ?_⟩ <;>
    (try simp_all [Function.LeftInverse, Function.RightInverse, Function.comp]) <;>
    (try aesop) <;>
    (try
      {
        ext x
        simp_all [Function.LeftInverse, Function.RightInverse, Function.comp]
        <;> aesop
      }) <;>
    (try
      {
        ext x
        simp_all [Function.LeftInverse, Function.RightInverse, Function.comp]
        <;> aesop
      })

by
  apply Iff.intro
  · -- Prove the forward direction: if U ≃ V, then there exist inverses f and g
    intro h
    have h₁ : U ∼ V := h
    obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h₁
    refine' ⟨f, hf.symm, _, _⟩
    · -- Prove f ∘ hf.symm = id
      rw [Function.comp]
      ext x
      have h₂ := hf.bijective.surjective
      simp_all [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
    · -- Prove hf.symm ∘ f = id
      rw [Function.comp]
      ext x
      have h₂ := hf.bijective.injective
      simp_all [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
  · -- Prove the reverse direction: if there exist inverses f and g, then U ≃ V
    rintro ⟨f, g, hfg, hgf⟩
    refine' ⟨f, _⟩
    -- Show that f is an equivalence by proving it is bijective
    have h₁ : Function.Bijective f := by
      constructor
      · -- Prove injectivity
        intro x y hxy
        have h₂ := congr_fun hgf x
        have h₃ := congr_fun hgf y
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₂ h₃
        have h₄ : g (f x) = x := by simp_all
        have h₅ : g (f y) = y := by simp_all
        simp_all
      · -- Prove surjectivity
        intro y
        use g y
        have h₂ := congr_fun hfg y
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₂
        have h₃ : f (g y) = y := by simp_all
        simp_all
    exact h₁

by
  refine' ⟨fun h => ⟨h.toFun, h.invFun, _, _⟩, fun ⟨f, g, hfg, hgf⟩ => ⟨f, _⟩⟩
  <;> simp_all [Equiv.ext_iff, Function.ext_iff]
  <;> aesop

by
      apply Set.Subset.antisymm
      · -- Prove that {n : Int | even n} ⊆ Set.range (fun (n : Int) => 2 * n)
        intro x hx
        rw [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx
        have h₁ : x % 2 = 0 := by
          rw [Int.even_iff] at hx
          exact hx
        -- Since x is even, we can write x = 2 * (x / 2)
        have h₂ : x = 2 * (x / 2) := by
          have h₃ : x % 2 = 0 := h₁
          have h₄ : x = 2 * (x / 2) := by
            omega
          exact h₄
        -- Therefore, x is in the range of the function (fun n => 2 * n)
        rw [h₂]
        exact ⟨x / 2, by simp⟩
      · -- Prove that Set.range (fun (n : Int) => 2 * n) ⊆ {n : Int | even n}
        intro x hx
        rw [Set.mem_range] at hx
        rcases hx with ⟨n, rfl⟩
        -- For any x in the range, x = 2 * n, which is even
        rw [Int.even_iff]
        norm_num [Int.mul_emod]
        <;> simp [Int.emod_emod]
    -- Since the set of even integers is the range of an injective function from ℤ, it is denumerable
    rw [h]
    apply denum_range_of_injective
    intro a b h
    simp_all [mul_left_inj]
    <;> omega
  exact h_main

by
  constructor
  · -- Prove the forward direction: if U ≈ V, then there exist f : U → V and g : V → U such that f ∘ g = id and g ∘ f = id
    intro h
    rw [Equiv.exists] at h
    obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
    refine' ⟨f, hf.symm, _, _⟩
    · -- Prove that f ∘ g = id
      funext x
      simp_all [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
    · -- Prove that g ∘ f = id
      funext x
      simp_all [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
  · -- Prove the reverse direction: if there exist f : U → V and g : V → U such that f ∘ g = id and g ∘ f = id, then U ≈ V
    intro h
    rcases h with ⟨f, g, hf, hg⟩
    have h1 : ∀ x : U, g (f x) = x := by
      intro x
      have h2 : (g ∘ f) x = x := by rw [hg]
      simpa using h2
    have h2 : ∀ y : V, f (g y) = y := by
      intro y
      have h3 : (f ∘ g) y = y := by rw [hf]
      simpa using h3
    refine' Equiv.mk f g _ _
    · -- Prove that g is the inverse of f
      intro x
      exact h1 x
    · -- Prove that f is the inverse of g
      intro y
      exact h2 y

by
  constructor
  · intro h
    have h₁ : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := ⟨h⟩
    obtain ⟨e⟩ := h₁
    refine' ⟨e.toFun, e.invFun, _, _⟩
    · -- Prove f ∘ g = id
      funext x
      have h₂ : e.symm (e x) = x := by
        rw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
      simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
    · -- Prove g ∘ f = id
      funext x
      have h₂ : e (e.symm x) = x := by
        rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
      simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
  · intro h
    obtain ⟨f, g, h₁, h₂⟩ := h
    refine' ⟨Equiv.ofBijective f ⟨_, _⟩⟩
    · -- Prove f is injective
      refine' fun a b hab => _
      have h₃ := congr_fun h₂ a
      have h₄ := congr_fun h₂ b
      simp at h₃ h₄
      simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
    · -- Prove f is surjective
      intro y
      have h₃ := congr_fun h₁ y
      simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₃
      refine' ⟨g y, _⟩
      simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
